title: Help remains available for Sarasota County homeowners dealing with damage from 2024 hurricane season
summary: A local non-profit is urging Sarasota County homeowners to reach out for help as they continue to offer assistance and recovery from the 2024 hurricane season.

title: Suspect accused of brutally beating, disfiguring man at Bartlett Park arrested for attempted murder
summary: St. Petersburg police arrested a 49-year-old man for attempted second-degree murder after he allegedly beat a victim at a local park and stole from his backpack.

title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. visits Tampa General Hospital to launch healthy food pledge
summary: U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and USDA Secretary Brooke Rollins visited Tampa General Hospital on Thursday to roll out a nationwide push aimed at transforming how medical facilities feed their patients.

title: South Tampa trap caught 18,000 mosquitoes, last month just 142
summary: Hillsborough County conducted aerial spraying early Thursday to treat a growing mosquito population – a sudden influx that’s tied to the Bay Area’s recent drought.

title: Donalds, Jolly hold the inside track as Florida governor’s race enters final primary stretch
summary: With Florida’s Aug. 18 primary approaching, the race for governor is increasingly centered on two frontrunners, Republican U.S. Rep. Byron Donalds and Democrat David Jolly.

title: More police patrols coming to New Port Richey roads as safety campaign targets crashes on US 19
summary: The New Port Richey Police Department is increasing traffic enforcement as part of a partnership with the Florida Department of Transportation's Enhanced Law Enforcement Engagement (ELEE) program.

title: Epstein survivor demands Blanche be held accountable for DOJ identity exposure in the files
summary: Epstein survivor Dani Bensky says her name, phone number and addresses were published in the Epstein Files — despite her attorneys submitting 350 names for redaction in advance. Now she wants answers.

title: Florida fentanyl summit draws teens, grieving parents and DEA chief as crisis takes center stage
summary: They came with questions — and answers — about how fentanyl spreads and why young people may not recognize the danger.

title: Proposed St. Pete budget includes increase for public safety funding
summary: The City of St. Pete could soon be expanding mental health services, starting with the CALL Program.

title: Search suspended for 3 missing after boat with family spreading ashes capsizes on San Francisco Bay
summary: The U.S. Coast Guard has suspended its search for three people missing after a boat capsized near Alcatraz Island in San Francisco Bay. One person died after being pulled from the water.

title: National Intelligence confirmation hearing highlights continued controversy around the 2020 election
summary: The Trump administration remains closely focused on election security and the outcome of the 2020 election. National Intelligence nominee Jay Clayton dodged questions about it on Wednesday.

title: 2 of 8 men charged in alleged plot to attack the White House UFC event plead not guilty
summary: Two of the eight men indicted in an alleged drone and sniper plot to attack President Donald Trump's UFC cage-fighting show on the White House lawn pleaded not guilty to federal conspiracy charges.

title: Alternative bill to permanent daylight saving time gains support from doctors, lawmakers
summary: Doctors and lawmakers are backing a proposal for permanent standard time as an alternative to year-round daylight saving time.

title: This rule could deny green cards to immigrants who use public benefits
summary: The Trump administration is reviving a rule that could deny green cards to immigrants who use public benefits that could include food stamps, Medicaid, housing vouchers and others.

title: Sen. Mark Kelly wants more men in classrooms through new legislation
summary: Sen. Mark Kelly is promoting bipartisan legislation aimed at recruiting more teachers and expanding mentorship opportunities for young men.

Clearwater City Council to vote on 30-year Duke Energy deal
summary: After years of analysis into whether Clearwater should operate its own electric utility, city leaders are expected to take up a proposal Thursday to extend the city's partnership with Duke Energy.

title: Chatbots could be at risk of spreading government restrictions on online speech
summary: Ask Claude to make a pamphlet critical of President Donald Trump or Britain's King Charles III, and Anthropic's chatbot would oblige.

title: Texas floodwaters surge toward Camp Mystic area one year after deadly floods
summary: Texas crews raced to rescue stranded residents as dangerous flooding surged near Camp Mystic, reviving memories of last year’s deadly disaster.

title: NYT files motion to quash subpoenas served over Air Force One coverage
summary: David McCraw, the newspaper's senior vice president and deputy general counsel, accused the government of bringing the subpoenas in "bad faith to punish The Times for its coverage."

title: Pluto flyby mission wakes up after long sleep nearly 6 billion miles from Earth
summary: A groundbreaking mission that explored Pluto and distant solar system objects in unprecedented detail has awakened from its longest sleep ever.

title: Blue states hit hardest by Trump disaster aid denials, analysis says
summary: AP analysis finds Trump is taking longer to approve disaster aid, with denials disproportionately affecting Democratic-led and blue states.

title: Blue Angels jet flies too close to crowd at Pensacola Beach
summary: A US Navy Blue Angels jet flew too low over a crowd at Pensacola Beach in Florida on Wednesday, July 15.

title: Lumber spill shuts down southbound Interstate 75 in Hillsborough: FHP
summary: A tractor-trailer driver was cited on Thursday after the lumber he was hauling spilled onto Interstate 75 in Hillsborough County, troopers said.
